stories.title: LeBron James Decision Day — Moratorium Lifts, Clock Is Ticking

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
TODAY IS THE DAY.

The NBA moratorium lifts at 12:01 PM ET — and LeBron James can finally sign.

Rich Paul: the decision won't come "in the next few days."

Warriors. Cavaliers. Timberwolves. The wait continues. 👑 #NBAFreeAgency

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
12:01 PM ET passed. LeBron James is officially eligible to sign.

Still no announcement. Rich Paul says be patient.

Warriors signed Porzingis. Cavs are waiting. Wolves made their pitch.

Where does LeBron land? #NBAFreeAgency

article.headline: LeBron James Decision Day: Moratorium Lifts, But the Wait Isn't Over Yet

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
LeBron James Decision Day

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Search term:** `LeBron James Cleveland Cavaliers` OR `LeBron James Golden State Warriors`
- **Usage:** X + Facebook post
- **Notes:** Game action or portrait; no text overlay needed

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
- **Search term:** `LeBron James Los Angeles Lakers`
- **Usage:** Article featured image
- **Notes:** Clean portrait or game action; landscape orientation

---

research.facts:
LeBron James Decision Day
**Source 1 — NBA.com / ESPN:**
- LeBron James has informed the Lakers he plans to play elsewhere in 2026-27 (confirmed)
- The NBA moratorium lifts July 6 at 12:01 PM ET — first day any free agent can officially sign
- LeBron (41yo) entering record-breaking 24th NBA season
**Source 2 — Rich Paul / NBC Sports Bay Area / Yahoo Sports:**
- Rich Paul told reporters the LeBron decision won't come "in the next few days"
- "He's heard from every team in the entire league except one or two" — Rich Paul
- Paul indicated patience, suggesting weeks rather than days
**Source 3 — Bleacher Report / Stadium Rant / ibtimes.com.au:**
- Warriors and Cavaliers remain clear frontrunners on prediction markets
- Minnesota Timberwolves made a "surprise pitch" over the weekend
- Warriors signed Kristaps Porzingis while awaiting LeBron decision (NBC Sports Bay Area)
- Warriors building roster for max LeBron/AD scenario
**Confidence:** HIGH (multiple independent sources on moratorium date, Rich Paul quote, LeBron informing Lakers)

stories.title: The Big Executions — Giannis, Kawhi, Brown Trades All Become Official

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
IT'S OFFICIAL — as of 12:01 PM ET today:

🔥 Giannis Antetokounmpo is a Miami Heat
🍁 Kawhi Leonard is a Toronto Raptor
🔔 Jaylen Brown is a Philadelphia 76er
💚 Paul George is a Boston Celtic

The NBA offseason just became real. #NBA

article.headline: It's Official: Giannis in Miami, Kawhi in Toronto, Brown in Philly — the NBA's Offseason Becomes Real

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
The Big Executions — Giannis, Kawhi, Brown Trades Official

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Search term:** `Giannis Antetokounmpo Milwaukee Bucks`
- **Usage:** X + Facebook post
- **Notes:** Expressive action shot; dominant framing

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
- **Search term:** `Jaylen Brown Boston Celtics`
- **Usage:** Article featured image
- **Notes:** Portrait or celebration shot

---

research.facts:
Giannis, Kawhi, Brown/George Trades Execute Officially
**Source 1 — NBC Sports Bay Area / Wikipedia / Olympics.com Trade Tracker:**
- July 6 at 12:01 PM ET: moratorium lifts, all pre-agreed trades officially execute
- Giannis Antetokounmpo + Bobby Portis → Miami Heat
- Milwaukee receives: Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakučionis, No. 13 2026 draft pick, 2031 and 2033 first-round picks, 2030 pick swap, 2033 second-round pick
**Source 2 — Olympics.com / CBS Sports / Yahoo Sports:**
- Kawhi Leonard → Toronto Raptors
- Clippers receive: Brandon Ingram, Gradey Dick, two first-round picks, two second-round picks, one pick swap
- Kawhi at 34 returning to Toronto for second stint
**Source 3 — Bleacher Report / CBS Sports / Blazer's Edge:**
- Jaylen Brown (2024 Finals MVP) → Philadelphia 76ers
- Paul George + 2028 unprotected first + 2031 unprotected first + 2028 second + 2030 second → Boston Celtics
- All three trades execute at 12:01 PM ET on July 6
**Confidence:** HIGH (confirmed across multiple credible sports outlets)

research.facts:
Summer League Day 2 — California Classic
**Source 1 — ESPN / Bleacher Report:**
- July 5, 2026 California Classic results (Day 2 of Summer League)
- Los Angeles Lakers 93, Miami Heat 91 (OT) — Cameron Carr 26 points, Anton Watson game-winner
- Golden State Warriors Gold 98, San Antonio Spurs 69 — Yaxel Lendeborg strong showing
- Sacramento Kings 91, Golden State Warriors Blue 85 — Emanuel Sharp led comeback, Kings now 2-0
**Source 2 — NBA.com:**
- Salt Lake City Summer League continues Day 2 as well
- California Classic running concurrently in Bay Area
- 2026 draft class continues to generate buzz from Day 1 (Peterson, Boozer)
**Confidence:** HIGH (game scores confirmed via ESPN box scores)

stories.title: Warriors' Master Plan — Porzingis Signed, Anthony Davis Trade Pursued

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The @warriors aren't just waiting for LeBron.

They've signed Kristaps Porzingis. They're pursuing Anthony Davis from Washington.

Golden State is building the roster FIRST — then asking LeBron to come win.

Smart strategy? Or too aggressive? #NBA #Warriors

article.headline: The Warriors' Grand Plan: Porzingis Signed, Anthony Davis Trade in Pursuit

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)

- **Usage:** Article featured image

---

research.facts:
Warriors' Master Plan — Porzingis + Anthony Davis Pursuit
**Source 1 — NBC Sports Bay Area / Bleacher Report / ClutchPoints:**
- Warriors signed Kristaps Porzingis (confirmed, NBC Sports Bay Area free agency recap)
- Warriors simultaneously pursuing Anthony Davis trade from Washington Wizards
- Davis trade package would need to include Jimmy Butler (expiring $57M contract) + draft capital
- Warriors have two future firsts and four first-round swaps available
**Source 2 — CBS Sports / Bleacher Report:**
- Washington Wizards re-signed Trae Young (4yr/$212M) and signaled intent to keep Davis
- Davis trade to Warriors faces significant hurdles
- Warriors' primary goal remains signing LeBron James first, then pursuing Davis
**Confidence:** MEDIUM (Porzingis signing confirmed, Davis pursuit confirmed but Washington resistance noted)

stories.title: Veteran Signings Go Official — Drummond to Knicks, Powell to Bulls

NBA free agency Day 1 official signings are in:

Andre Drummond → Knicks (1yr/$3.8M)
Norman Powell → Bulls (2yr deal)
Tari Eason → Rockets (5yr/$81.5M officially signed)

The league is being remade, one contract at a time. #NBA #NBAFreeAgency

article.headline: Day 1 Official: Drummond Joins Knicks, Powell Signs with Bulls, Eason Rockets Deal Finalized

research.facts:
Veteran Free Agency Day 1 — Official Signings
**Source 1 — Blazer's Edge / CBS Sports / Spotrac:**
- Andre Drummond signs Knicks 1yr/$3.8M–$3.9M (figures vary slightly by source)
- Norman Powell signs Chicago Bulls 2-year deal
- James Harden reportedly negotiating with Cleveland Cavaliers (from 07-05 notes)
**Source 2 — Multiple outlets:**
- July 6 is Day 1 of official contract signings (moratorium lifts 12:01 PM ET)
- Dozens of agreed-upon deals officially become binding starting today
- Tari Eason's 5yr/$81.5M Rockets deal also officially executes today
**Confidence:** MEDIUM (Drummond Knicks, Powell Bulls confirmed; exact Drummond dollar figure varies $3.8M vs $3.9M — use $3.8M per Blazer's Edge/CBS)
